paper_title: Characteristics of Strains and Data Acquisition

methods.description: Process for preparing and cultivating monoclonal strains of the genus Frustulia for analysis.

steps.description: Mount strains onto permanent slides using Naphrax.
steps.searchable_summary: mount strains onto slides with Naphrax

steps.description: Cultivate strains in slightly acidic, oligotrophic liquid medium enriched with sodium metasilicate at 18°C with continuous lighting.
steps.searchable_summary: cultivate strains in specific medium and conditions

steps.description: Harvest cells in the exponential phase of growth and clean frustules by incineration.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest and clean frustules by incineration

methods.description: Procedure for photographing and processing images of strains for analysis.

steps.description: Photograph strains using an Olympus BX51 with differential interference contrast imaging.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of strains with Olympus BX51

steps.description: Unify the orientation of cells and cut off the redundant background using Adobe Photoshop 2020.
steps.searchable_summary: process images to unify orientation and remove background

methods.description: Method for registering shapes of strains using landmarks for analysis.

steps.description: Digitize 50 images per strain using tpsUtil ver. 1.6.
Register shapes by 96 landmarks using tpsDig ver. 2.3 in CW and CCW orientations.
steps.searchable_summary: register shapes with landmarks using tpsDig
